title: tui: send_chat broadcasts plaintext even when mls_active — relay reads all TUI messages
description: tui/src/event.rs send_chat() (line 956) builds a plaintext BroadcastParams payload and sends it via rpc_methods::BROADCAST with no encryption regardless of state.mls_active. The comment on line 955 says 'MLS encryption will be added when mls_active' but this was never implemented. Consequence: every TUI chat message is visible to the relay server, negating the E2E encryption guarantee that CLI users have. The CLI's main send path uses HPKE-sealed MLS ciphertext (sealed broadcast) when mls_active. Fix: in send_chat, when state.mls_active is true, encrypt via state.mls_client.encrypt() + pad(), then HPKE-seal to state.room_hpke_secret, and send via rpc_methods::SEALED_BROADCAST as the CLI does.
